agent_capability_concepts.definition: The filler must be a human agent capable of contextual, ethical, and legally binding decisions.
agent_capability_concepts.scope_note: Used for ApprovalGate steps where requiresHumanApproval is true. AI agents may not fill roles with this capability.

agent_capability_concepts.definition: The filler must be capable of probabilistic risk scoring over structured datasets.
agent_capability_concepts.scope_note: Can be satisfied by an AIAgent with an appropriate model version (e.g. risk-classifier-v2.4.1).

agent_capability_concepts.definition: The filler must have authority to certify regulatory and contractual compliance.
agent_capability_concepts.scope_note: Requires a human agent. Cannot be delegated to AI or automated pipeline.

agent_capability_concepts.definition: The filler must be an automated pipeline capable of running a deterministic build, test, and deploy sequence.
agent_capability_concepts.scope_note: Satisfied by AutomatedPipelines. HumanAgents should not fill roles requiring only this capability.

agent_capability_concepts.definition: The filler must hold executive authority to approve high-impact organizational changes.
agent_capability_concepts.scope_note: Reserved for the top of delegation chains. Maps to the ntwf:delegatesTo escalation pattern.

artifact_type_concepts.definition: A human- or AI-authored analytical document (e.g. a risk assessment report).
artifact_type_concepts.scope_note: Use for narrative analytical artifacts, not for raw datasets or machine logs.

artifact_type_concepts.definition: An artifact recording an approval or authorization decision (e.g. a release authorization, a legal clearance).
artifact_type_concepts.scope_note: Use for sign-off / clearance / authorization artifacts.

artifact_type_concepts.definition: A machine-generated record of an automated activity (e.g. a deployment log).
artifact_type_concepts.scope_note: Use for deterministic pipeline/system output, not human analysis.

automated_pipelines.name: CI/CD Deploy Pipeline
automated_pipelines.description: Automated build, test, and deployment pipeline for production releases. Deterministic execution — no probabilistic output.

change_log.terms_affected: Initial release: 9 classes, 13 object properties, 5 datatype properties, 2 CBox schemes.
change_log.rationale: First validated release of the NTWF workflow ontology; passes the 104-test suite.

change_log.motivating_question: Which AI agent was executing this step on March 1, 2026?
change_log.terms_affected: Added filledBy assignment history (RoleAssignments) with validity periods; AIAgent deployment date; agent-type-change audit witnesses.
change_log.rationale: Operational governance needed retained filledBy history so historical 'as of date' and blast-radius queries are answerable without discarding provenance. Additive and backward compatible — a minor, backward-compatible change, so the steward is authorized to approve and deploy it without authority review.

competency_questions.name: steps-and-order
competency_questions.question_text: What are all the steps in the release process, and in what order do they execute?
competency_questions.explanation: WorkflowSteps ordered by SequencePosition; the order is the transitive closure of the asserted StepPrecedence edges, materialized as ntwf:precedesStep.

competency_questions.name: who-approves
competency_questions.question_text: Who or what is responsible for approving a production deployment?
competency_questions.explanation: The Release Approval Gate (step 3) -> its AssignedRole (Release Manager) -> FilledByHumanAgent. The role-agent indirection is resolved by the GateApproverHuman lookup.

competency_questions.name: ai-vs-human-steps
competency_questions.question_text: Which steps are executed by AI agents, and which require human decision?
competency_questions.explanation: Each step's ExecutingAgentType (via its role's filler type) classifies AI vs pipeline vs human; RequiresHumanApproval marks the human-decision steps. CountAISteps aggregates the AI side.

competency_questions.name: artifact-lineage
competency_questions.question_text: What artifacts does the review produce, and what consumes them downstream?
competency_questions.expected_answer: 5 artifacts in a wasDerivedFrom chain: Risk Report -> Legal Clearance -> Release Authorization -> Deployment Log -> Post-Deployment Report
competency_questions.explanation: WorkflowArtifacts.ProducedByStep gives the producer; DerivedFromArtifact gives the PROV wasDerivedFrom parent; RequiredBySteps gives the downstream consumer.

competency_questions.name: stale-over-12-months
competency_questions.question_text: Which workflows have not been reviewed or updated in twelve months?
competency_questions.expected_answer: No - Production Deployment is not stale (MonthsSinceModified 2 <= 12)
competency_questions.explanation: IsStale = MonthsSinceModified > StalenessThresholdMonths. The seed workflow was modified 2 months ago against a 12-month threshold, so it is not stale.

competency_questions.name: escalation-path
competency_questions.question_text: What happens when the VP of Engineering is unavailable during escalation?
competency_questions.expected_answer: Escalates Release Manager -> VP of Engineering -> Chief Technology Officer
competency_questions.explanation: Roles.DelegatesTo is a transitive chain. With the VP of Engineering unavailable, the next hop in the closure is the CTO.

competency_questions.name: engineering-and-legal
competency_questions.question_text: Which workflows involve both Engineering and Legal?
competency_questions.expected_answer: Yes - Production Deployment involves both (Engineering-owned steps and Legal-owned steps)
competency_questions.explanation: InvolvesEngineeringAndLegal = AND(CountEngineeringOwnedSteps > 0, CountLegalOwnedSteps > 0). Step 2 is Legal-owned; steps 1/3/4/5 are Engineering-owned.

competency_questions.name: datasets-and-ai
competency_questions.question_text: What datasets does the review consume, and which AI agents processed them?
competency_questions.expected_answer: Q1 2026 Risk Metrics, consumed by the AI Risk Assessment step and processed by RiskAnalysis-AI
competency_questions.explanation: Datasets.ConsumedBySteps (inverse of WorkflowSteps.ConsumesDataset) gives the consuming step; that step's role filler resolves the processing AI agent.

conformance_tests.name: sweep-changelog-vs-answer-key

conformance_tests.name: sweep-vocabularyreconciliations-vs-answer-key
conformance_tests.explanation: Every row and every column of VocabularyReconciliations must match the Postgres-oracle answer key on both engines.

conformance_tests.explanation: Every row and every column of Scenarios must match the Postgres-oracle answer key on both engines.

conformance_tests.name: inferred-precedence-1-to-5
conformance_tests.explanation: The headline inference: 4 asserted edges entail the never-asserted 1→5 pair via transitive precedesStep.

conformance_tests.name: inferred-delegation-release-manager-to-cto
conformance_tests.display_name: Inferred delegation Release Manager to CTO
conformance_tests.explanation: Escalation chain RM→VP Eng→CTO: the RM→CTO hop is never asserted; transitive delegatesTo entails it.

conformance_tests.name: both-engines-compute-identical-answers
conformance_tests.explanation: The multi-substrate equivalence claim: reasoner and Postgres views disagree on zero computed values.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-iri-minted
conformance_tests.explanation: Every row is a globally-addressable individual; the workflow's IRI is derived, stable, and identical on both engines.

conformance_tests.name: approval-gate-iri-minted
conformance_tests.explanation: Gate IRI derives through the step's path chain (lookup → calc chain).

conformance_tests.name: step-1-executing-agent-type-resolves
conformance_tests.explanation: Role→agent indirection: the step's executing agent type is derived through AssignedRole→FilledBy*.

conformance_tests.name: gate-step-executing-agent-type-resolves
conformance_tests.display_name: Gate step executing agent type resolves
conformance_tests.explanation: The approval-gate step resolves to its human Release Manager through the same one-edge chain.

conformance_tests.name: ai-step-count
conformance_tests.explanation: COUNTIFS over the DERIVED IsExecutedByAI child field — conditional aggregation over a computed column.

conformance_tests.name: human-step-count
conformance_tests.explanation: The human side of the agent mix.

conformance_tests.name: hasaiagentstep-boolean
conformance_tests.explanation: Boolean derived from the AI step count — an aggregation feeding a calc.

conformance_tests.name: gate-step-is-human-filled-at-seed
conformance_tests.display_name: Gate step is human-filled at seed
conformance_tests.explanation: requiresHumanApproval is satisfied at seed: the gate role is filled by a human.

conformance_tests.name: no-approval-violation-at-seed
conformance_tests.explanation: The consistency rule does NOT fire on the seed facts — the clean baseline the mutation test breaks.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-has-no-violation-at-seed
conformance_tests.display_name: Workflow has no violation at seed
conformance_tests.explanation: Workflow-level rollup of approval violations is false on seed facts.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-status-is-a-skos-concept
conformance_tests.display_name: Workflow status is a SKOS concept
conformance_tests.explanation: workflowStatus points into the WorkflowStatusConcepts scheme.

conformance_tests.name: role-capability-is-a-skos-concept
conformance_tests.display_name: Role capability is a SKOS concept
conformance_tests.explanation: hasCapability points into the AgentCapabilityConcepts scheme.

conformance_tests.name: role-carries-label-documentation
conformance_tests.explanation: Heuristic 6: label/comment documentation travels with the model into every substrate.

conformance_tests.name: gate-escalation-threshold
conformance_tests.explanation: The gate's defining datatype property (raw, gate-only).

conformance_tests.name: gate-role-resolves-via-lookup
conformance_tests.explanation: INDEX/MATCH lookup gate→step→role. KNOWN GAP CANDIDATE: both engines currently drop lookup columns from their individuals payload — this test exists to keep that gap visible until fixed.

conformance_tests.name: gate-approver-human-resolves-via-lookup
conformance_tests.display_name: Gate approver human resolves via lookup
conformance_tests.explanation: Second-order lookup gate→role→human filler. Same known gap candidate as GateRole.

conformance_tests.name: release-manager-has-exactly-one-filler
conformance_tests.display_name: Release Manager has exactly one filler
conformance_tests.explanation: Functional filledBy: exactly one of the three filler arms is set.

conformance_tests.name: no-roles-with-bad-filler-cardinality
conformance_tests.display_name: No roles with bad filler cardinality
conformance_tests.explanation: Suite-4 cardinality witness: zero roles violate the exactly-one-filler rule on seed facts.

conformance_tests.name: release-authorization-derives-from-legal-clearance
conformance_tests.display_name: Release authorization derives from legal clearance
conformance_tests.explanation: One link of the 5-artifact PROV wasDerivedFrom chain.

conformance_tests.name: risk-report-attributed-to-the-risk-ai
conformance_tests.display_name: Risk report attributed to the risk AI
conformance_tests.explanation: PROV attribution: the artifact names the AI agent that produced it.

conformance_tests.name: post-deploy-report-has-a-derivation-parent
conformance_tests.display_name: Post-deploy report has a derivation parent
conformance_tests.explanation: Boolean derived from the lineage link.

conformance_tests.name: step-consumes-the-dcat-dataset
conformance_tests.explanation: consumesDataset points at the dcat:Dataset; answers CQ8.

conformance_tests.name: legal-step-is-legal-owned
conformance_tests.explanation: ownedBy chain step→role→department resolves to Legal.

conformance_tests.name: deploy-step-is-engineering-owned
conformance_tests.explanation: Same chain resolving to Engineering.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-involves-engineering-and-legal
conformance_tests.explanation: The cross-department join CQ7 asks about.

conformance_tests.name: step-4-sequence-position
conformance_tests.explanation: The resolved SequencePosition (override-if-pinned, else PrecedingStepCount+1) computes identically in both substrates; step 4 has four transitive predecessors and no override, so it resolves to 4.

conformance_tests.name: step-1-duration-minutes
conformance_tests.explanation: Drives the time-budget bar; raw datatype property.

conformance_tests.name: asserted-precedence-pair-count
conformance_tests.explanation: 4 asserted edges.

conformance_tests.name: inferred-precedence-pair-count
conformance_tests.explanation: 6 pairs exist only by inference (10-pair closure minus 4 asserted).

conformance_tests.name: total-precedence-closure-pairs
conformance_tests.explanation: The 10-pair transitive closure, counted from the materialized closure rows.

conformance_tests.name: months-since-last-review
conformance_tests.explanation: Time-dependent calc (months granularity) — the staleness clock.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-not-stale-at-seed
conformance_tests.explanation: The CQ5 governance query on the seed facts.

conformance_tests.name: stale-and-has-ai-at-seed
conformance_tests.explanation: The higher-compliance-risk join on the seed facts.

conformance_tests.name: authority-can-approve-changes
conformance_tests.explanation: Steward vs Authority: only the Authority approves.

conformance_tests.name: steward-cannot-approve-changes
conformance_tests.explanation: The other half of the governance split.

conformance_tests.name: change-1.0.0-breaking-change-flag
conformance_tests.explanation: Semantic-versioning derivation from ChangeKind.

conformance_tests.name: change-1.1.0-backward-compatible-flag
conformance_tests.explanation: The minor release is back-compat by derivation.

conformance_tests.name: current-role-assignment-flagged
conformance_tests.explanation: Bitemporal validity: the open-ended assignment is current.

conformance_tests.name: assignment-active-as-of-audit-date
conformance_tests.display_name: Assignment active as of audit date
conformance_tests.explanation: The 'who filled this on the audit date' time-travel query.

conformance_tests.name: agent-type-change-detected
conformance_tests.explanation: AI→Human turnover detection across consecutive assignments.

conformance_tests.name: turnover-requires-compliance-audit
conformance_tests.explanation: The audit trigger derived from the agent-type change.

conformance_tests.name: workflow-compliance-audit-rollup
conformance_tests.explanation: Workflow-level count of audit-triggering changes.

conformance_tests.name: risk-ai-attributed-artifact-count
conformance_tests.explanation: AI-system-registry blast radius: artifacts this AI produced.

conformance_tests.name: risk-ai-impacted-workflow-count
conformance_tests.explanation: Blast radius: workflows reachable from this AI's artifacts.

conformance_tests.name: risk-ai-model-version
conformance_tests.explanation: The audit anchor: which model version produced the attributed artifacts.

conformance_tests.name: risk-ai-deployment-date
conformance_tests.explanation: When the audited model version went live.

conformance_tests.name: assign-ai-to-the-human-only-gate-fires-the-violation
conformance_tests.display_name: Assign AI to the human-only gate fires the violation
conformance_tests.explanation: Suite-4 inject-an-error: filling the gate role with an AI must flip ApprovalConsistencyViolation on the step AND roll up to the workflow, on BOTH engines.

conformance_tests.name: backdating-the-review-makes-the-workflow-stale
conformance_tests.display_name: Backdating the review makes the workflow stale
conformance_tests.explanation: Backdate dct:modified past the staleness window: IsStale must flip true.

conformance_tests.name: backdating-fires-the-stale-and-ai-compliance-join
conformance_tests.display_name: Backdating fires the stale-AND-AI compliance join
conformance_tests.explanation: Seed facts already include AI-executed steps, so staleness alone completes the IsStaleAndHasAIAgent join.

conformance_tests.name: dropping-the-sign-off-requirement-clears-the-violation
conformance_tests.display_name: Dropping the sign-off requirement clears the violation
conformance_tests.explanation: The violation is an AND of requiresHumanApproval and a non-human filler: with the requirement off, an AI filler is consistent.

governance_roles.name: ontology-steward
governance_roles.responsibilities: Monitors the ontology for drift, tracks external-dependency updates (PROV-O, FOAF, Dublin Core, DCAT, Schema.org), fields questions from users, maintains documentation, and keeps the validation suite current. Identifies that a change is needed but does not approve it. Runs the periodic (at minimum quarterly) competency-question review: new unanswerable questions become scope-change requests, obsolete questions become deprecation candidates, and questions that return wrong answers are defects.

governance_roles.name: ontology-authority
governance_roles.responsibilities: Holds the power to approve changes to the CBox, ABox, and TBox. Decides how and where a change is made. Reviews changes proposed by the steward so that no single person alters the knowledge graph's semantic integrity unchecked.

role_assignments.name: ntwf-deployment-health-role [2026-02-05 -> 2026-02-20]
role_assignments.reason: compliance reassignment: automated task assigned to a human for execution

role_assignments.name: ntwf-deployment-health-role [2026-02-20 -> open]
role_assignments.reason: model upgrade: returned to automated summarization (health-summarizer-v1.2.0)

roles.name: ci/cd-executor
roles.label: CI/CD Executor
roles.comment: Executes the automated build, test, and deployment sequence. Filled by an AutomatedPipeline. Demonstrates CQ3: this step does NOT require human approval.

roles.name: chief-technology-officer
roles.label: Chief Technology Officer
roles.comment: Top of the delegation chain. Has cap-executive-authorization. When both Release Manager and VP Engineering are unavailable, the CTO is the final escalation target.

roles.name: deployment-health-agent
roles.label: Deployment Health Agent
roles.comment: Generates the post-deployment health report by summarizing telemetry after a release. A role, not an identity — currently filled by the DeploymentHealth-AI agent (health-summarizer-v1.2.0), it could be reassigned to a human or a newer model without changing the workflow structure. Its RoleAssignments history records an earlier human-reviewer period and the agent-type transitions.

roles.name: vp-of-engineering
roles.label: VP of Engineering
roles.comment: First escalation target when Release Manager is unavailable. Demonstrates ntwf:delegatesTo chain: Release Manager → VP Engineering → CTO.

roles.name: risk-analysis-agent
roles.label: Risk Analysis Agent
roles.comment: Performs probabilistic risk scoring over the Q1 risk metrics dataset. This is a role, not an identity — filled by RiskAnalysis-AI today but could be replaced by a newer model version without changing the workflow structure.

roles.name: legal-compliance-reviewer
roles.label: Legal Compliance Reviewer
roles.comment: Certifies that the deployment plan meets regulatory and contractual requirements. Must be a human agent — AI agents may not fill this role (requiresHumanApproval).

roles.name: release-manager
roles.label: Release Manager
roles.comment: Owns the production deployment process. Fills the Release Approval Gate (step 3): the human authority who explicitly authorizes the release before the automated deployment runs. This is the CQ2 answer — gate -> Release Manager -> Maria Gonzalez. Top of the delegation chain (Release Manager -> VP Engineering -> CTO).

scenario_cq_effects.name: reroute-ordering-cq-1
scenario_cq_effects.note: Re-pointing prec-4-5 shrinks the precedence closure (10 → 7 pairs); positions recompute. No other answer moves.

scenario_cq_effects.name: ai-release-manager-cq-2
scenario_cq_effects.note: Release Manager filled by AI → the gate has no human approver.

scenario_cq_effects.name: ai-release-manager-cq-3
scenario_cq_effects.note: Same edit: the gate's step (3) is now AI-executed, so the AI-step count rises 2 → 3. Cannot be avoided — the gate role is a step executor.

scenario_cq_effects.name: human-risk-step-cq-3
scenario_cq_effects.note: Risk Analysis role handed to a human → AI-step count falls 2 → 1. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: break-derivation-cq-4
scenario_cq_effects.note: Legal Clearance loses its wasDerivedFrom parent → the PROV chain splits. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: stale-docs-cq-5
scenario_cq_effects.note: Modified date backdated past the window → IsStale flips true. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: break-delegation-cq-6
scenario_cq_effects.note: Release Manager → VP edge removed → the escalation closure collapses. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: legal-to-engineering-cq-7
scenario_cq_effects.note: Legal role re-owned by Engineering → zero legal-owned steps → eng-AND-legal flips No. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: dataset-unconsumed-cq-8
scenario_cq_effects.note: Dataset consumed-by link cleared → no step consumes it. Isolated.

scenario_cq_effects.name: trigger-risk-cq-5
scenario_cq_effects.note: Backdates the review date → IsStale true (while AI steps remain — the narrative risk).

scenario_cq_effects.name: all-human-cq-3
scenario_cq_effects.note: Every AI/pipeline role reassigned to a human → AI-step count falls to 0.

scenario_cq_effects.name: ai-at-gate-cq-3
scenario_cq_effects.note: Legal review step handed to AI → AI-step count rises 2 → 3 (the headline effect is the consistency-violation witness, not a CQ).

scenarios.name: stale-workflow-with-an-ai-step
scenarios.label: Stale workflow with an AI step
scenarios.explanation: Backdates the workflow's last review past its 12-month policy window so the docs go STALE. The release already has AI-executed steps, so stale-docs-AND-an-AI-step fires the article's headline business query: IsStaleAndHasAIAgent (CQ5 extended — stale workflows that involve AI agents).

scenarios.name: all-human-release
scenarios.label: All-human release
scenarios.explanation: Reassigns every AI agent and pipeline to a human. The AI-step count drops to zero, so the compliance risk can no longer fire — even if the docs are stale. Shows the role→agent indirection: personnel change is one edge, not a rewrite.

scenarios.name: ai-at-the-approval-gate
scenarios.label: AI at the approval gate
scenarios.explanation: Hands the Legal Compliance Reviewer role — whose review step requires human sign-off — to an AI agent. The reasoner's consistency witness fires on that step: a requires-human-approval step is no longer human-filled. The rule break is DERIVED, not hand-checked.

scenarios.name: reset-to-baseline
scenarios.label: Reset to baseline
scenarios.explanation: Restores the canonical seed assignments and a fresh review date — the known-good starting point. Every role is filled by exactly one agent, no requires-human-approval step is AI-filled, and the workflow is within its staleness window, so all the article's clean-ABox witnesses read green.

scenarios.name: re-route-the-last-ordering-edge
scenarios.label: Re-route the last ordering edge
scenarios.explanation: Re-points the final precedence edge so step 5 follows step 1 instead of step 4. The transitive closure shrinks (10 → 7 ordered pairs) and the inferred positions recompute — nothing else moves. Demonstrates CQ1: order is a projection of the edges.

scenarios.name: ai-fills-the-release-gate
scenarios.label: AI fills the release gate
scenarios.explanation: Hands the Release Manager role — the human authority on the approval gate — to an AI agent. The gate approver is no longer human (CQ2), and because that role also executes step 3, the AI-step count ticks up (CQ3). The two answers cannot be decoupled: the gate's approver IS a step executor.

scenarios.name: hand-the-risk-step-to-a-human
scenarios.label: Hand the risk step to a human
scenarios.explanation: Reassigns the Risk Analysis role from its AI agent to a human. The AI-executed step count drops from 2 to 1 — and only that. Demonstrates CQ3: the count is a live rollup over which steps are AI-executed, not a stored number.

scenarios.name: break-the-provenance-chain
scenarios.label: Break the provenance chain
scenarios.explanation: Clears the wasDerivedFrom link on the Legal Clearance Certificate. The PROV derivation chain splits in two — the downstream artifacts can no longer trace back to the risk report. Demonstrates CQ4: derivation is an asserted edge, and the chain answer follows it.

scenarios.name: let-the-docs-go-stale
scenarios.label: Let the docs go stale
scenarios.explanation: Backdates the workflow's last-modified date past its staleness window. IsStale flips to true — and only that derived flag. Demonstrates CQ5: staleness is MonthsSinceModified vs the threshold, recomputed against the clock, never asserted.

scenarios.name: cut-the-escalation-chain
scenarios.label: Cut the escalation chain
scenarios.explanation: Removes the Release Manager → VP Engineering delegation edge. The escalation chain (and its transitive closure) collapses to the unreachable tail. Demonstrates CQ6: delegation is a first-class edge, and the chain answer is its closure.

scenarios.name: move-legal-review-under-engineering
scenarios.label: Move legal review under engineering
scenarios.explanation: Re-owns the Legal Compliance role from the Legal dept to Engineering. With zero legal-owned steps, the cross-cutting 'involves engineering AND legal' check flips to No. Demonstrates CQ7: the boolean is an AND over department-owned step counts, derived from role ownership.

scenarios.name: detach-the-risk-dataset
scenarios.label: Detach the risk dataset
scenarios.explanation: Clears the dataset's consumed-by-step link. The Q1 Risk Metrics dataset is no longer consumed by any step. Demonstrates CQ8: dataset consumption is an asserted relationship, and the answer is exactly which steps point at it.

vocabulary_reconciliations.name: foaf:name owl:sameAs ntwf:name
vocabulary_reconciliations.rationale: Worked example: a deprecated FOAF element is re-prefixed into the NTWF namespace; existing data stays valid (URIs do not disappear), and foaf:name owl:sameAs ntwf:name keeps the two interchangeable for reasoners.

vocabulary_reconciliations.name: dcat:Dataset (v2) owl:sameAs dcat:Dataset (v3)
vocabulary_reconciliations.rationale: External-dependency fact: DCAT went through a major v2 -> v3 revision that introduced new classes and deprecated others. NTWF tracks the alignment so its dcat:Dataset usage stays semantically current; existing data remains valid because URIs do not disappear.

workflow_status_concepts.definition: Workflow is being authored or reviewed and is not yet in production use.
workflow_status_concepts.scope_note: Set this status when the workflow has not completed its initial review cycle.

workflow_status_concepts.definition: Workflow is approved and currently being executed by the organization.
workflow_status_concepts.scope_note: Only active workflows should be assigned to running process instances.

workflow_status_concepts.definition: Workflow has been replaced by a newer version and should not be used for new instances.
workflow_status_concepts.scope_note: Deprecated workflows may still have in-flight instances; do not archive until all instances close.

workflow_status_concepts.definition: Workflow is no longer in use and has been moved to historical records.
workflow_status_concepts.scope_note: Archived workflows answer CQ5 stale-workflow queries but generate no new instances.

workflows.name: production-deployment
workflows.title: Production Deployment Workflow
workflows.description: The primary NTWF ABox example. A 5-step workflow for deploying software to production at Talisman's Special Solutions. Steps involve a human Release Manager (step 1), an AI Risk Analysis Agent (step 2), a human Legal Compliance Reviewer at an Approval Gate (step 3), an automated CI/CD pipeline (step 4), and the Release Manager again for the post-deployment report (step 5). Demonstrates all three agent types, the delegation chain, artifact provenance, and DCAT dataset consumption.
